ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a masterpiece and a key film in the debate between directors Bong Joon-ho and Park Chan-wook. Considered one of the best modern films.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Highly praised as potentially the best film from both Bong Joon-ho and Park Chan-wook's filmographies, with several comments highlighting its excellence.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as one of Park Chan-wook's best works, often compared favorably to Parasite in the director debate.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as one of the best films ever seen by commenters and a standout in Park Chan-wook's filmography.

ranked_movies.title: Decision to Leave
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as another excellent film by Park Chan-wook, compared to Parasite and Memories of Murder in quality.

ranked_movies.title: Mother
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as one of Bong Joon-ho's top-notch films alongside Parasite and Memories of Murder.

ranked_movies.title: The Chaser
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in relation to The Yellow Sea, considered arguably a better (though harder to watch) film by Na Hong-jin.

ranked_movies.title: The Yellow Sea
ranked_movies.reason: The main subject of a highly-upvoted post, praised for its brutal action, fantastic score, and twisted story.

ranked_movies.title: Save the Green Planet!
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended multiple times as an underappreciated Korean movie that deserves more love. Also mentioned as the source material for the upcoming Yorgos Lanthimos film Bugonia.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an awesome movie by Kim Jee-woon alongside The Good, The Bad and The Weird.

ranked_movies.title: The Good, The Bad and The Weird
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as an awesome movie by Kim Jee-woon, recommended alongside I Saw the Devil.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in a Star Trek discussion about an episode title being a play on this South Korean zombie movie.

ranked_movies.title: A Dirty Carnival
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie that deserves more love.

ranked_movies.title: Sea Fog
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as an underappreciated Korean film worth watching.

ranked_movies.title: Welcome to Dongmakgol
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a potentially underappreciated Korean film, though the commenter wasn't sure if it's already loved or not.

ranked_movies.title: Asura: The City of Madness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie from 2016.

ranked_movies.title: Decibel
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a Korean movie the commenter really liked and saw in theatre.

ranked_movies.title: Peppermint Candy
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an older Korean film (1999) that left a strong impression on the viewer.

ranked_movies.title: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: Although not explicitly mentioned, it's part of Park Chan-wook's celebrated Vengeance trilogy alongside Oldboy and Lady Vengeance, which would likely be discussed in the broader context of his filmography.

ranked_movies.title: Lady Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: Like Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance, this completes Park Chan-wook's Vengeance trilogy and would be part of the discussion around his consistent quality and versatility mentioned by commenters.
